nodes.label: { analyzeAndSuggestRoles, matchRolesToMarket, buildCareerPath, analyzeGaps, selectTopGaps, chatWithCoach }
nodes.norm_label: { analyzeandsuggestroles, matchrolestomarket, buildcareerpath, analyzegaps, selecttopgaps, chatwithcoach }

nodes.label: Layer separation rule: knowledge/agents/plumbing never mix
nodes.rationale: Change one layer (knowledge/, agents/, core+routes+render) without touching the others, to avoid re-tangling responsibilities the refactor deliberately separated.
nodes.norm_label: layer separation rule: knowledge/agents/plumbing never mix

nodes.label: Information flow: User to Coach to HR to User-decides to CV Writer
nodes.rationale: User overrides HR's Add/Leave-out lean per gap; Coach's takeaway only flows forward, kept separate from CV Writer; Researcher/Curator enrich discipline knowledge in background, decoupled from the synchronous user flow.
nodes.norm_label: information flow: user to coach to hr to user-decides to cv writer

nodes.label: Business case: fast CV tailoring + apply flow
nodes.rationale: Primary goal is making job applications as fast and easy as possible: upload CV, find US jobs filtered by state, rank by fit, tailor CV, export to Word, apply directly.
nodes.norm_label: business case: fast cv tailoring + apply flow

nodes.label: JSearch poor US/state coverage blocker
nodes.rationale: JSearch API has no reliable US state-level filtering, blocking the core business flow; resolved later by switching to Jooble per the bug backlog note.

nodes.label: Switch to Jooble with US state filtering
nodes.rationale: Resolves the country/location filter bug by replacing JSearch with Jooble, which supports reliable US state-level geo-filtering.
nodes.norm_label: switch to jooble with us state filtering

nodes.label: GDPR/privacy backlog: stop persisting client CVs
nodes.rationale: Uploaded CVs persist on disk (services/uploads.js multer storage), tailored CVs in output/, raw CV text in session state — none encrypted/access-controlled/auto-deleted; must be resolved before real client data flows through, and before PayPal payment integration (#19) is built.
nodes.norm_label: gdpr/privacy backlog: stop persisting client cvs

nodes.label: Word template options (4-choice picker)
nodes.rationale: Default/Alternate built-in generators, disabled 'Similar to original CV' (blocked on PDF-only readCV()), and an AI-placement engine (docxPlacement.js/planDocxPlacement) that only decides placement, never invents wording, for plain uploaded Word templates vs deterministic docxtemplater merge-tag templates.

nodes.label: Default gapSeverities to major only
nodes.rationale: clientPreferences.gapSeverities currently defaults to all three severities; backlog wants default narrowed to ['major'] only so minor/mild gaps become opt-in.

nodes.label: Unanswered gap-confirmation defaults to skip
nodes.rationale: If user tailors CV without responding to an Accept/Skip/Discuss gap item, treat as skip; the HR/recruiter agent (not a hardcoded rule) should judge per-gap whether silent omission is safe given severity and rationale.

nodes.label: STATUS.md upkeep rule
nodes.rationale: STATUS.md must be rewritten and staged into the same commit as the work that changed state, never a separate 'update status' commit, so the status board stays accurate per package.

nodes.label: Target architecture layering plan (Part B)
nodes.rationale: Proposes splitting the monolithic ai.js/server.js/templates.js into knowledge/, agents/, tasks/, core/, services/, render/, routes/ so a feature = ~1-file change instead of touching multiple god-files.

nodes.label: Two-layer self-improving knowledge (Part D)
nodes.rationale: Universal hand-written rules (knowledge/recruiter-core.md) plus per-discipline auto-built/self-improving JSON stores (knowledge/disciplines/<field>.json), curated via confidence-weighted merge so recurring skills rise and noise decays; user-pinned items never decay and override search.

nodes.label: Discipline knowledge learning loop
nodes.rationale: Curator loads existing discipline store; if new/stale/refresh-checked, Researcher web-searches and Curator merges with confidence bump on repeats; user-routed items apply with pinned highest weight; rendered rubric feeds Recruiter.

nodes.label: UI integration plan (Part E) — Input Router + refresh checkbox
nodes.rationale: Reuses the existing ci-instructions comment box: Input Router classifies free text as field-agnostic (recruiter-core) vs field-specific (disciplines/<field>), tagged source:user/pinned:true; a new ci-refresh-discipline checkbox controls live-search cost, default auto-checked only when field is new/stale.
nodes.norm_label: ui integration plan (part e) — input router + refresh checkbox

nodes.label: Eval harness measurement plan (Part H)
nodes.rationale: 3-5 real CV+job eval cases with sketched good output, diffed before/after any rubric/agent change, automated by the prompt-tester subagent, so 'better' is falsifiable rather than a feeling.

nodes.label: Refactor phase status table (phases 0-8, all done)
nodes.norm_label: refactor phase status table (phases 0-8, all done)

nodes.label: Researcher agent scoped as no-op stub
nodes.rationale: agents/researcher.js is a documented no-op stub with zero network calls until explicitly enabled later, to avoid live-search cost/risk during the refactor.
nodes.norm_label: researcher agent scoped as no-op stub

nodes.label: Per-phase test gate: test:ui + test:unit (mocked, free)
nodes.rationale: npm test (real API) costs money and is unmocked, so the per-phase gate uses the mocked test:ui and test:unit suites; the costly real-API suite runs only occasionally or at the end.
nodes.norm_label: per-phase test gate: test:ui + test:unit (mocked, free)

nodes.label: Open thread: verify /__dbcheck Postgres logging then remove it
nodes.rationale: Temporary GET /__dbcheck route (commit 99e24d0) reuses the real logger DB pool to confirm Postgres event logging actually writes rows before the route is reverted, closing the long-standing 'is logging real?' question tied to commit b91d829's validation-nudge logging.
nodes.norm_label: open thread: verify /__dbcheck postgres logging then remove it

nodes.label: Error popup split by kind: validation vs error
nodes.rationale: validation (missing input/wrong order) shows a friendly nudge with no code/red/support line, while error (real failure) shows a full technical dialog; single choke point core/respondError.js, sanitized logger.js allowlist widened to code/kind only, no PII.
nodes.norm_label: error popup split by kind: validation vs error

nodes.label: TRIAL_MODE config flag
nodes.rationale: core/config.js TRIAL_MODE defaults true; served via GET /config.js as window.TRIAL_MODE; shows the error code as a muted caption under validation nudges when trial mode is on.

nodes.label: Backlog: verify discipline learning loop fires per search
nodes.rationale: Only one discipline file exists (rf-hardware-engineering-technical-program-management.json); STATUS flags this as 'verify, not build' — confirm whether Researcher→Curator actually fires and updates files or is built-but-dormant.
nodes.norm_label: backlog: verify discipline learning loop fires per search

nodes.label: Mode B — market/scrape mode backlog cluster
nodes.norm_label: mode b — market/scrape mode backlog cluster

nodes.label: extractJSON() helper
nodes.rationale: Haiku 4.5 adds preamble/postamble text around JSON output; extractJSON() finds the first/last bracket pair to extract valid JSON regardless of surrounding text, replacing fragile regex-strip-only parsing.

nodes.label: normalizeLinkedin() helper
nodes.rationale: pdf2json extracts only the visible username text, not the href, so LinkedIn profile links appear as bare usernames; normalizeLinkedin() reconstructs the full https://www.linkedin.com/in/<username> URL from any partial form.

nodes.label: Coordinate-based PDF text joining
nodes.rationale: pdf2json stores text as discrete positioned tokens; naive .join(' ') misjoins them. Using the x/y gap between consecutive tokens (gap < 0.5 and same line) decides whether to join with no space or a space, fixing cases like underscores being dropped (hadi_emadi -> hadiemadi) and email/LinkedIn corruption.

nodes.label: confirmedContact session override pattern
nodes.rationale: Because pdf2json can silently corrupt sensitive fields (name/email/phone/LinkedIn) by dropping characters like underscores, the app captures user-verified contact info once via a contact review card and stores it in appSession.confirmedContact as the highest-priority override during CV generation, instead of re-extracting from the PDF every time.

nodes.label: Interactive editable tailored CV (contenteditable)
nodes.rationale: Tailored CV HTML fields use contenteditable=true with .sl single-line class plus Enter-key prevention, and plain-text-only paste via clipboardData getData('text/plain') + execCommand('insertText'), so the candidate can manually polish wording with no further AI involvement and without breaking the CV layout.

nodes.label: DAILY_AI_BUDGET_USD / rate-limit env vars
nodes.rationale: Render deploy config caps daily AI spend ($3), AI rate limit (20), general rate limit (100/15min), and daily job search cap (50) — cost/abuse controls for the free-tier deployment.
